title: Narcissus citrinus
intro: Narcissus citrinus, also known as the bitter narcissus or yellow narcissus, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and bitter taste. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and possess antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus citrinus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or extract, and is used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Narcissus citrinus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. It is a bulbous perennial native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This species is often classified as a dwarf or miniature form of the common narcissus, and is known for its small, bright yellow flowers. Narcissus citrinus is also sometimes referred to as the 'desert daffodil' due to its ability to thrive in dry, arid environments. As a member of the Amaryllidaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the snowdrops and lily of the valley.
uses: Narcissus citrinus, a plant known for its bright yellow flowers, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, the plant's extracts are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant's essential oils may have antimicrobial effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Furthermore, the plant's flowers and bulbs contain compounds that may help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Narcissus citrinus for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Narcissus citrinus, also known as the yellow trumpet narcissus, is a type of flowering plant that is native to South Africa. While the plant is not typically used for medicinal purposes, it's essential to note that its bulb contains alkaloids and glycosides, which can be toxic if ingested in large quantities. Ingesting or handling the plant can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and eye problems. Additionally, the plant's sap can cause phototoxicity when exposed to sunlight, leading to skin blisters and rashes. It's recommended to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting any parts of it.
